sound_type: explosive bark -- sharp percussive 'WRAHH!' 500-2,000 Hz. 0.2-0.5 sec. Territory, alarm, contact. E=4
ecosystem: Varied. Forest edges, agricultural fields, suburban neighborhoods, urban margins. Boundary specialist. Resident year-round.
season: Year-round. Bark peaks in breeding season (January-February).
family: Canidae (fourth entry -- coyote 208, coyote pup 209, gray wolf 210)

coordinates.description: Red Fox in varied PNW habitats: forest edges, agricultural fields, suburban margins.

taxonomy_note.description: 140-degree callback from degree 71. Fourth Canidae. CANIDAE QUARTET COMPLETE (208-211). Most adaptable canid.
taxonomy_note.family_count_in_engine: Canidae: Coyote Howl (208, E=5), Coyote Pup (209, E=4), Gray Wolf (210, E=7), Red Fox (211, E=4). COMPLETE.
taxonomy_note.name_etymology: Vulpes vulpes = 'fox fox'. Redundant binomial. The most fox-like fox.
taxonomy_note.genus_note: Vulpes -- the true foxes. Red Fox is the type species.
taxonomy_note.order_note: Carnivora. Fourth Canidae entry. Quartet complete.
taxonomy_note.family_position: Canidae: fourth entry. Quartet complete. Energy arc: 5-4-7-4.
taxonomy_note.class_note: Class Mammalia. Most widely distributed wild carnivore on Earth. Boundary specialist.

song_metrics.song_frequency_hz: Bark: 500-2,000 Hz. Alarm bark: 800-2,500 Hz. Breeding scream: 1,000-3,000+ Hz.
song_metrics.song_duration_s: Bark: 0.2-0.5 sec. Repeated every 3-10 seconds in territorial bouts.
song_metrics.note_structure: Single explosive burst. Percussive, not tonal. No sustain.
song_metrics.note_types: Territorial bark (loud, explosive), alarm bark (sharp, higher), contact bark (soft, brief), breeding scream (drawn-out wail).
song_metrics.phrase_structure: Single barks repeated. No chorus (solitary hunter). Scream is single sustained call.
song_metrics.timbre: Sharp, explosive, percussive. Abrupt onset and offset. No resonant sustain.
song_metrics.amplitude: E=4. Explosive but brief. High instantaneous energy, low sustained. Flash not glow. 200-500 m carry.
song_metrics.tempo: Barks every 3-10 seconds during territorial bouts.
song_metrics.context: Territory advertisement. Alarm. Contact. Breeding (bark + scream). Year-round.
song_metrics.regional_variation: Subtle variation in bark pitch across populations. Scream consistent.
song_metrics.key_property: RED FOX BARK. Sharp explosive. 500-2,000 Hz. E=4. 140-DEGREE CALLBACK from 71. Fourth Canidae. QUARTET COMPLETE. Boundary specialist. v1.49.352.

pelage.overall: Classic red-orange coat with white-tipped tail. 4-7 kg. Slender build. Large ears. Bushy tail.
pelage.diagnostic_mark: Red-orange coat. White tail tip (diagnostic). Black stockings. Pointed face. Large ears relative to head.
pelage.seasonal_variation: Thicker winter coat with richer color. Summer coat thinner, more faded.

physical_description.overall: 4-7 kg (9-15 lbs). Body length 45-90 cm. Tail 30-55 cm. Slender, agile. Built for versatility.
physical_description.diagnostic_mark: Red-orange fur. White-tipped bushy tail. Black legs/stockings. Pointed face. Cat-like pupils (vertical slit in bright light).
physical_description.field_id: Medium-small red-orange canid with white-tipped tail. Black stockings. Often seen at dusk/dawn at habitat edges. May bark sharply if disturbed. Cat-like in movement -- light, quick, solitary.

nodes.title: Canidae Quartet COMPLETE -- 208-211
nodes.description: Four canid voices. Energy arc 5-4-7-4. Habitat: everywhere, den, wilderness, edges. Size: medium, tiny, large, small. Conservation: common, common, extirpated, resident.

nodes.title: 140-Degree Callback -- Fox Returns
nodes.description: From degree 71 to 211. More than a third of the 360. The fox bridges time within the engine itself. Longest callback in current stretch.

nodes.title: Boundary Specialist -- Most Adaptable Canid
nodes.description: Thrives where habitats meet. Forest edge, field edge, suburban edge. Dietary flexibility. Behavioral adaptability. Every parameter tunable.

nodes.title: Percussive vs. Tonal -- Bark vs. Howl
nodes.description: Fox bark: percussive, 0.2-0.5 sec, flash. Wolf howl: tonal, 3-11 sec, glow. Two fundamental approaches to canid vocalization.

cross_references.relationship: 140-DEGREE CALLBACK! First fox entry. Returns 140 degrees later with full comparative context.
CANIDAE CONTRAST! Wolf (E=7, sustained, landscape) vs. fox (E=4, percussive, territory). Apex to compact. -3 drop.
SAME ENERGY! Both E=4. Pup chorus (sustained, chaotic, group) vs. fox bark (percussive, brief, solitary).
BRIDGE BETWEEN WORLDS! Iska Dhaaf crosses cultural boundaries. Red Fox crosses ecological boundaries. Both thrive at edges.
